title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Delayed Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts · Inference Brief: Consumer Timeout Contract
summary: A design copies old TTL dead-letter retry loops into RabbitMQ 4.3, uses priority as unlimited preemption, leaves consumer timeouts unrelated to handler deadlines, and assumes new broker behavior exists on older clusters. Planned artifact: Add and verify the consumer timeout contract increment in RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Align broker consumer timeout, handler deadline, dependency timeout, cancellation, redelivery, and channel recovery without duplicate terminal decisions.
Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Consumer Timeout Contract · Theory Consumer Timeout Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 2977 in a recovery drill with stale callbacks from an old channel generation. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum retry, priority, and timeout behavior on version and valid budgets; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then change one routing segment and prove the exact destination set. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Consumer Timeout Contract contract for the supplied data.
